chapter_title.en: Chapter on Those to Whom Judgment is Permissible and Those to Whom it is Not Permissible

text: 3218 - وَ رَوَى عَطَاءُ بْنُ اَلسَّائِبِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا كُنْتُمْ فِي أَئِمَّةِ جَوْرٍ فَاقْضُوا فِي أَحْكَامِهِمْ وَ لاَ تَشْهَرُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ فَتُقْتَلُوا وَ إِنْ تَعَامَلْتُمْ بِأَحْكَامِنَا كَانَ خَيْراً لَكُمْ ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3218 - Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib narrated from Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) who said:
"When you are under oppressive rulers, then judge according to their rulings and do not expose yourselves, lest you be killed. However, if you conduct your dealings according to our rulings, it will be better for you."
